name: postgres.js (High-Performance PostgreSQL Client for Node.js)

auth.notes: Username/password; SSL/TLS; connection string or config object

performance.notes: Fastest PostgreSQL Node.js driver; 2-3x faster than node-postgres (pg) in benchmarks

agent_readiness.notes: High-performance PostgreSQL client. import postgres from 'postgres'. const sql = postgres(process.env.DATABASE_URL). const users = await sql`SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ${userId}`. sql`INSERT INTO users ${sql({name, email})}`. sql`UPDATE users SET ${sql(updates)} WHERE id = ${id}`. Tagged template literals for safe parameterization. Transactions: await sql.begin(async sql => { await sql`INSERT...` }). Cursors: for await (const [row] of sql`SELECT...`.cursor()). Listen/Notify: sql.listen('channel', cb). Type serialization. Connections pooled by default. COPY: sql`COPY ... FROM STDIN`. Prepared statements: sql`SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ${'1'}`.prepare('get-user').

gotchas: Tagged template literal syntax requires care — never interpolate with string concatenation
TypeScript: result type is Row[] not strongly typed — use generic sql<User[]>
Named parameters not supported — positional only via template literals
Transactions with savepoints require nested sql.begin()
ssl: 'require' needed for cloud PostgreSQL (AWS RDS, Railway, Supabase)
best_when: When pg (node-postgres) latency is a bottleneck
Simple SQL-first Node.js backend without ORM overhead
Streaming large result sets via cursor
avoid_when: Complex ORM features (use Drizzle, Prisma, or Kysely)
When strong TypeScript row typing is critical (use Kysely)
summary: postgres.js is the fastest Node.js PostgreSQL client. const sql = postgres(url); await sql`SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ${id}`. Tagged template literals for parameterization. Transactions, cursors, LISTEN/NOTIFY. Type-annotated. MIT by Rasmus Porsager. Best for high-performance SQL-first Node.js backends. 2-3x faster than pg. PostgreSQL only. Use Drizzle/Kysely for type-safe query builder. Never interpolate with string concat.
